name: Panfried Quail
recipeIngredient: 1 cup White grapes (to make 1/4 - 1/3 cup grape juice)
1/2 cup Virginia ham, cut into 2 by 1/4 inch matchsticks
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the fresh white grape juice: Crush the cup of grapes in a pestle, then press through a sieve or vegetable mill to extract the juice, or use a potato ricer.
2. Combine the salt, pepper, and thyme, crushing the thyme with your fingertips. Sprinkle both sides of the quail with the seasonings.
3. Melt the butter in a large skillet over medium heat until it foams and just begins to brown. Add the quail, skin-side down. Sprinkle with ham, cover, and cook for 3 to 4 minutes, until the skin is golden brown.
4. Turn the birds and continue cooking, covered, until the juices run clear, about 4 minutes longer. Remove the pan from the heat and let the quail rest, covered, for about 10 minutes.
5. Arrange the quail on a platter and sprinkle the ham from the pan over them.
6. Pour off the fat from the pan. Add the grape juice (you can also use water), and bring to a boil. Cook for 1 minute, scraping the browned bits from the bottom to deglaze the pan. Pour over the quail and serve.
7. Serves 4.
This southern recipe was created by Edna Lewis for Gage and Tollner, a Brooklyn restaurant. Mrs. Lewis is a black lady who's been active at restaurants in North and South Carolina. She grew up in Freetown, VA and is the author of three cookbooks.
